revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24744680692|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Horowasan Haushiwake-jinja]][[ja:保呂羽山波宇志別神社]] {{Expand Japanese|保呂羽山波宇志別神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Horowasan Haushiwake Shrine | native_name = 保呂羽山波宇志別神社 | image = Haushibetsu Shrine Kagura-den.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Horowasan Haushiwake Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|39.37111|140.310124}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ankan|lt=Ankan|ar|الإمبراطور أنكان|arz|الامبراطور انكان|azb|آنکان (ایمپیراتور)|cs|Ankan|de|Ankan|en|Emperor Ankan|es|Ankan Tennō|fa|امپراتور آنکان|fi|Ankan|fr|Ankan|hu|Ankan japán császár|id|Kaisar Ankan|it|Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|ka|ანკანი|ko|안칸 천황|mr|अंकन|nl|Ankan|pl|Ankan|pt|Ankan|ru|Император Анкан|sh|Car Ankan|simple|Emperor Ankan|sl|Cesar Ankan|sv|Ankan (kejsare)|th|จักรพรรดิอังกัง|tl|Emperador Ankan|tr|Ankan (imparator)|uk|Імператор Анкан|uz|Imperator Ankan|vi|Thiên hoàng An Nhàn|yo|Emperor Ankan|zh_min_nan|Ankan Thian-hông|zh_yue|安閑天皇|zh|安闲天皇|qid=Q241765|1=Emperor Ankan}} }} {{nihongo|'''Horowasan Haushiwake Shrine'''|保呂羽山波宇志別神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Dewa Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11395784|ltq=Q11395784|qid=Q11395784|1=List of Shikinaisha in Dewa Province}} ** Series ordinal: 8 ** P155: {{ill|Shioyuhiko Shrine|lt=Shioyuhiko Shrine|ja|塩湯彦神社|qid=Q11428793|1=Shioyuhiko Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135040055|ltq=Q135040055|qid=Q135040055|1=Sohikahano Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Yokote, Akita|lt=Yokote|ar|يوكوته (أكيتا)|azb|یوکوته، آکیتا|ceb|Yokote-shi|ce|Йокоте|cs|Jokote (Akita)|de|Yokote|en|Yokote, Akita|envoyage|Yokote|eo|Jokote|es|Yokote (Akita)|et|Yokote|fa|یوکوته، آکیتا|fi|Yokote|fr|Yokote|gl|Yokote|id|Yokote, Akita|it|Yokote|ja|横手市|javoyage|横手市|kk|Йокоте қаласы|ko|요코테시|mg|Yokote|mzn|یوکوته، آکیتا|nl|Yokote|pl|Yokote|pt|Yokote|ro|Yokote|ru|Йокоте|sh|Jokote|sr|Јокоте|sv|Yokote|tg|Ёкоте|th|โยโกเตะ|tl|Yokote, Akita|tr|Yokote|tt|Йокоте|uk|Йокоте|ur|یوکوتے، اکیتا|uz|Yokote (Akita)|vi|Yokote, Akita|war|Yokote, Akita|zh_classical|橫手市|zh_min_nan|Yokote-chhī|zh_yue|橫手|zh|橫手市|zhvoyage|橫手|qid=Q496479|1=Yokote, Akita}} * {{ill|Q876032|ltq=Q876032|qid=Q876032|1=Dewa Province}} * {{ill|Hiraka District, Akita|lt=Hiraka district|en|Hiraka District, Akita|ja|平鹿郡|qid=Q5768935|1=Hiraka District, Akita}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|39.37111|140.310124}} == image (P18) == * Haushibetsu Shrine Kagura-den.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Emperor Ankan|lt=Ankan|ar|الإمبراطور أنكان|arz|الامبراطور انكان|azb|آنکان (ایمپیراتور)|cs|Ankan|de|Ankan|en|Emperor Ankan|es|Ankan Tennō|fa|امپراتور آنکان|fi|Ankan|fr|Ankan|hu|Ankan japán császár|id|Kaisar Ankan|it|Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|ka|ანკანი|ko|안칸 천황|mr|अंकन|nl|Ankan|pl|Ankan|pt|Ankan|ru|Император Анкан|sh|Car Ankan|simple|Emperor Ankan|sl|Cesar Ankan|sv|Ankan (kejsare)|th|จักรพรรดิอังกัง|tl|Emperador Ankan|tr|Ankan (imparator)|uk|Імператор Анкан|uz|Imperator Ankan|vi|Thiên hoàng An Nhàn|yo|Emperor Ankan|zh_min_nan|Ankan Thian-hông|zh_yue|安閑天皇|zh|安闲天皇|qid=Q241765|1=Emperor Ankan}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 182145 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|1=Ken-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * ほろわさんはうしわけじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7410005005211 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '秋田県横手市大森町八沢木字保呂羽山1-1', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Horowasan Haushiwake-jinja}} [[JA:保呂羽山波宇志別神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11385699}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Horowasan Haushiwake Shrine |画像=[[File:Haushibetsu Shrine Kagura-den.jpg|270px|Haushibetsu Shrine Kagura Hall (national Important Cultural Property)]] |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Akita Prefecture|Q81863|qid=Q81863|1=Akita Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yokote|Q496479|qid=Q496479|1=Yokote, Akita}}{{ill|lt=Ōmori-machi (Akita)|Q8081626|qid=Q8081626|lt=Ōmori-machi|1=Ōmori, Akita}}Yasawagi Aza Horowasan 1-1 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|39|22|16.34|N|140|18|35.54|E|region:JP-05_scale:30000_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Horowasan Haushiwake Shrine}}| |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|qid=Q241765|1=Emperor Ankan}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=757|Q31135|qid=Q31135|1=AD 757}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō Hōji|Q1346307|qid=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 1) |本殿 = Double {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=August 15|Q2774|qid=Q2774|1=August 15}}, {{ill|lt=November 7|Q2989|qid=Q2989|1=November 7}} |神事 = Horowasan Shimotsuki Kagura |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Akita|label=Horowasan
Haushiwake
Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Horowasan Haushiwake Shrine''' (ほろわさんはうしわけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} on the slope of {{ill|lt=Mount Horowa|Q11385697|qid=Q11385697|1=Mount Horowa}} in Yasawagi, Ōmori-machi, {{ill|lt=Yokote|Q496479|qid=Q496479|1=Yokote, Akita}}, {{ill|lt=Akita Prefecture|Q81863|qid=Q81863|1=Akita Prefecture}}. It is identified with the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=shikinaisha small shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} "Haushiwake Shrine" of Hiraka District, {{ill|lt=Dewa Province|Q876032|qid=Q876032|1=Dewa Province}}, and its former rank was prefectural shrine. === Origin === According to shrine tradition, in {{ill|lt=757|Q31135|qid=Q31135|1=AD 757}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō Hōji|Q1346307|qid=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 1), Ōtomo Yoshichika invited {{ill|lt=Zaō Gongen|Q10514059|qid=Q10514059|1=Zaō Gongen}} of {{ill|lt=Kinpusen|Q10563986|qid=Q10563986|1=Mount Kinpusen}} in Yoshino, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|qid=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, and founded the shrine at the current site. Of the three shikinaisha in Akita Prefecture, this is the only one continuously surviving without interruption. In the medieval period it drew reverence as a sacred site of Shugendō. According to records held by the Ōtomo family, since foundation the Ōtomo family, said to descend from the {{ill|lt=Ōtomo clan|Q17227938|qid=Q17227938|1=Ōtomo clan (ancient)}}, served as {{ill|lt=bettō|Q2900274|qid=Q2900274|1=Bettō}}. In the early Edo period, because the Ōtomo head was a child, bettō were temporarily provided from both the Ōtomo and Moriya families. In {{ill|lt=1853|Q7666|qid=Q7666|1=1853}} ({{ill|lt=Kaei|Q1146125|qid=Q1146125|1=Kaei}} 6), a fire from the Moriya family residence caused deaths of lodging pilgrims and loss of domain-issued documents; Moriya Sagenji was punished by exile. The Moriya family line continued via adopted heir Moriya Mikinoshin with shrine duties, but after he was summoned in Meiji 2 (March 27) from priestly office to near-retainer service, the Moriya family moved to Kubota castle town. Since then to the present, the Ōtomo family has served as {{ill|lt=chief priest|Q11453500|qid=Q11453500|1=Gūji}}. (Separate lineage traditions are also preserved in the now-defunct-as-priestly Moriya family.) In the early modern period, as one of the three major shrines of the Akita domain designated by the {{ill|lt=Satake clan|Q2975363|qid=Q2975363|1=Satake clan}}, it received strong reverence; the bettō of Haushiwake Shrine also served as head of domain shrine houses. National studies flourished there, with Ōtomo Chikahisa studying under {{ill|lt=Motoori Norinaga|Q384647|qid=Q384647|1=Motoori Norinaga}} and Ōtomo Naoe under {{ill|lt=Motoori Ōhira|Q3325272|qid=Q3325272|1=Motoori Ōhira}}, influencing regional trends in the late Edo period. A theory by {{ill|lt=Niino Naoyoshi|Q11504231|qid=Q11504231|1=Naoyoshi Niino}} suggests the original deity may have been {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Kami|Q10939697|qid=Q10939697|1=Otori-sama}}, but this is not among current enshrined deities. === Enshrined deities === {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|qid=Q241765|1=Emperor Ankan}} Co-enshrined: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|qid=Q1640658|lt=Homusubi-no-Kami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kikurihime-no-Kami|Q11619891|qid=Q11619891|lt=Kikurihime-no-Mikoto|1=Kikurihime}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|qid=Q455602|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|qid=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}, {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Kami|Q4387856|qid=Q4387856|lt=Kanayabiko-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}, {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|qid=Q719665|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Inari Ōkami}}, {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōnamochi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|qid=Q3275687}}, {{ill|lt=Ama-no-Iwato-wake-no-Kami|Q10940542|qid=Q10940542|lt=Iwato-wake-no-Kami|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}}, {{ill|lt=Omoikane|Q586088|qid=Q586088|lt=Yagokoro Omoikane-no-Mikoto|1=Omoikane}}, {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|qid=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi|qid=Q1107286}}, {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu-Motome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|qid=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|Q9150391|qid=Q9150391|qid=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|qid=Q386563|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}, {{ill|qid=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|qid=Q10935105|qid=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} === Festivals === * {{ill|lt=November 7|Q2989|qid=Q2989|1=November 7}}: {{ill|lt=Horowasan Shimotsuki Kagura|Q11385698|qid=Q11385698|1=Horowasan Shimotsuki Kagura}} (national {{ill|lt=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Q6573893|qid=Q6573893|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property}}) === Precinct-related sites === * Shimoi-dō (Fugen Bosatsu), etc. === Cultural properties === ; Buildings * Haushiwake Shrine Kagura Hall (national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|qid=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) === Chronology === (Items in 〈 〉 are related contextual events.) * {{ill|lt=757|Q31135|qid=Q31135|1=AD 757}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō Hōji|Q1346307|qid=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 1): Foundation * {{ill|lt=1602|Q6727|qid=Q6727|1=1602}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 7): 〈{{ill|lt=Satake Yoshinobu (Ukyō-daibu)|Q1071525|qid=Q1071525|lt=Satake Yoshinobu|1=Satake Yoshinobu}} enters Akita.〉 * {{ill|lt=1853|Q7666|qid=Q7666|1=1853}} ({{ill|lt=Kaei|Q1146125|qid=Q1146125|1=Kaei}} 6): Moriya family exiled ** On the night of Jan 3, despite a household death being concealed, pilgrims were lodged; a handlight ignited the donated b onden at the entrance, causing a major fire. Hundreds tried to escape but deep snow blocked all sides of the house, and over 300 died in the fire; responsibility led to exile''Shin Akita Sōsho'' Vol.7 p.2, Ishii Tadayuki, ''Izuen Chawa'' Vol.1. * {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|qid=Q7717|1=1868}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 1) March: 〈{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri decree|1=UNKNOWN}}〉 * {{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|qid=Q7746|1=1871}} (Meiji 4) May 14: 〈Establishment of {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|qid=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system〉 === Access === * 35 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=JR East|Q499071|qid=Q499071|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Ōmagari Station (Akita)|Q1052987|qid=Q1052987|lt=Ōmagari Station|1=Ōmagari Station (Akita)}} * 20 minutes from {{ill|lt=Akita Expressway|Q1060262|qid=Q1060262|1=Akita Expressway}} {{ill|lt=Ōmagari IC|Q10935675|qid=Q10935675|lt=Ōmagari IC|1=Omagari interchange}} via {{ill|lt=National Route 105|Q6156710|qid=Q6156710|1=Japan National Route 105}} * 30 minutes from {{ill|lt=Akita Expressway|Q1060262|qid=Q1060262|1=Akita Expressway}} {{ill|lt=Yokote-kita Smart IC|Q18458618|qid=Q18458618|lt=Yokote-kita SIC|1=Yokote-kita smart interchange}} via {{ill|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 29 Yokote-Ōmori-Ōuchi Line|Q11595657|qid=Q11595657|lt=Pref. Route 29|1=Akita Prefectural Road Route 29}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === {{参照方法|date=2018年8月|section=1}} [Reports] * Association for Preservation Technology of Cultural Heritage Architecture (ed.), ''Haushiwake Shrine Kagura Hall Preservation and Repair Work Report'', issued by Repair Committee for Important Cultural Property Haushiwake Shrine Kagura Hall, March 1993 * Akita Prefectural Museum (ed.), ''Akita Prefectural Museum Research Report No.40'', March 2015 [General books] * {{ill|lt=Niino Naoyoshi|Q11504231|qid=Q11504231|1=Naoyoshi Niino}} / Watanabe Tsunajirō, ''Early-modern National Studies of Haushiwake'', Ōmori Town, Nov 1996 [Encyclopedia] * {{ill|lt=Akita Sakigake Shimpō|Q11595779|qid=Q11595779|lt=Akita Sakigake|1=Akita Sakigake Shimpō}} (ed.), ''Akita Encyclopedia'', Sept 1981 === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Horowasan Shimotsuki Kagura|Q11385698|qid=Q11385698|1=Horowasan Shimotsuki Kagura}} === External links === * [https://www.city.yokote.lg.jp/shisetsu/1001528/1005081.html Facility Information: Horowa-no-Sato Museum (Ōmori)] - Yokote City *: (Exhibits include the Haushiwake Shrine Kagura Hall) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Horowasan Haushiwake Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Horowasan Haushiwake Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:横手市の歴史]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:横手市の建築物]]
